puzzles.firstSentence: It was a bright cold day in April, and the clocks were striking thirteen.
puzzles.author: George Orwell

puzzles.firstSentence: It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife.
puzzles.author: Jane Austen

puzzles.firstSentence: Call me Ishmael.
puzzles.author: Herman Melville

puzzles.firstSentence: In my younger and more vulnerable years my father gave me some advice that I've been turning over in my mind ever since.
puzzles.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

puzzles.firstSentence: It was the best of times, it was the worst of times, it was the age of wisdom, it was the age of foolishness, it was the epoch of belief, it was the epoch of incredulity, it was the season of Light, it was the season of Darkness, it was the spring of hope, it was the winter of despair.
puzzles.author: Charles Dickens

puzzles.firstSentence: All children, except one, grow up.
puzzles.author: J.M. Barrie

puzzles.firstSentence: Mr. and Mrs. Dursley, of number four, Privet Drive, were proud to say that they were perfectly normal, thank you very much.
puzzles.book: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
puzzles.author: J.K. Rowling

puzzles.firstSentence: In a hole in the ground there lived a hobbit.
puzzles.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

puzzles.firstSentence: It was a pleasure to burn.
puzzles.author: Ray Bradbury

puzzles.firstSentence: The past is a foreign country; they do things differently there.
puzzles.author: L.P. Hartley

puzzles.firstSentence: Happy families are all alike; every unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.
puzzles.author: Leo Tolstoy

puzzles.firstSentence: You don't know about me without you have read a book by the name of The Adventures of Tom Sawyer; but that ain't no matter.
puzzles.author: Mark Twain

puzzles.firstSentence: Someone must have slandered Josef K., for one morning, without having done anything truly wrong, he was arrested.
puzzles.author: Franz Kafka

puzzles.firstSentence: Ships at a distance have every man's wish on board.
puzzles.author: Zora Neale Hurston

puzzles.firstSentence: Once upon a time and a very good time it was there was a moocow coming down along the road and this moocow that was coming down along the road met a nicens little boy named baby tuckoo.
puzzles.book: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man
puzzles.author: James Joyce

puzzles.firstSentence: Many years later, as he faced the firing squad, Colonel Aureliano Buendía was to remember that distant afternoon when his father took him to discover ice.
puzzles.author: Gabriel García Márquez

puzzles.firstSentence: When he was nearly thirteen, my brother Jem got his arm badly broken at the elbow.
puzzles.author: Harper Lee

puzzles.firstSentence: If you really want to hear about it, the first thing you'll probably want to know is where I was born, and what my lousy childhood was like, and how my parents were occupied and all before they had me, and all that David Copperfield kind of crap, but I don't feel like going into it, if you want to know the truth.
puzzles.author: J.D. Salinger

puzzles.firstSentence: Last night I dreamt I went to Manderley again.
puzzles.author: Daphne du Maurier

puzzles.firstSentence: Whether I shall turn out to be the hero of my own life, or whether that station will be held by anybody else, these pages must show.
puzzles.author: Charles Dickens

puzzles.firstSentence: The sun shone, having no alternative, on the nothing new.
puzzles.author: Samuel Beckett

puzzles.firstSentence: There was no possibility of taking a walk that day.
puzzles.author: Charlotte Brontë

puzzles.firstSentence: They say when trouble comes close ranks, and so the white people did.
puzzles.author: Jean Rhys

puzzles.firstSentence: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
puzzles.author: Various Authors

puzzles.firstSentence: Stately, plump Buck Mulligan came from the stairhead, bearing a bowl of lather on which a mirror and a razor lay crossed.
puzzles.author: James Joyce

puzzles.firstSentence: It was love at first sight.
puzzles.author: Joseph Heller

puzzles.firstSentence: All this happened, more or less.
puzzles.author: Kurt Vonnegut

puzzles.firstSentence: Christmas won't be Christmas without any presents, grumbled Jo, lying on the rug.
puzzles.author: Louisa May Alcott

puzzles.firstSentence: A screaming comes across the sky.
puzzles.author: Thomas Pynchon
